rationale_for_baseline_tier: Painful but non-emergency skin infection treatable with topical therapy and self-care; needs prompt (24–48h) review/prescription and diabetes attention, with a clear secondary-cellulitis safety-net given diabetes.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever, redness spreading and hardening beyond the folds, or thick purulent discharge
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek urgent/same-day review; ED if systemically unwell
escalation_edges.rationale: Secondary bacterial cellulitis in a diabetic can progress to sepsis and needs antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a fever, the redness spreads and feels hard/hot, or you see thick yellow pus — get urgent medical care the same day.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Poorly controlled diabetes with skin breakdown
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for the cellulitis safety-net; prioritise glycaemic optimisation.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Large body habitus with deep folds
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise separation/drying of folds and loose clothing to prevent recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use the antifungal cream twice daily and the barrier paste, and keep the folds dry and separated. See your GP within a few days for review and diabetes checks. Watch for spreading hard redness, pus or fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 24–48h; urgent care/ED if cellulitis develops
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for advice; urgent care/ED for fever or spreading infection.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document candidal intertrigo with antibiotic/diabetes precipitants, treatment started, and request glycaemic review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care only with no cellulitis safety-net in a poorly controlled diabetic.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Secondary cellulitis develops in broken diabetic skin and progresses to sepsis before review.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without addressing precipitants or the diabetic infection risk.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending to ED for uncomplicated candidal intertrigo without any infection red flags.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot take scrapings/Wood's lamp or palpate for deep induration remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat clinically as candidal intertrigo with antifungal + drying/barrier care, address precipitants, and safety-net for cellulitis given diabetes.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using a potent steroid alone, or missing the diabetic cellulitis risk.
